memory.summary: The term project deadline creates immediate pressure, so I will skip leisure and tackle the quick chore first to clear my schedule for deep work.
That trivial chore was a negligible win; it barely moved the needle on my term project progress. I need to immediately pivot back to high-impact work rather than celebrating minor tasks.
This trivial chore yielded a negligible 0.05 progress gain; it was a distraction that barely offset my sleep debt. I must immediately pivot back to high-impact term project work.

The casual gratitude from my cohort is noted, but social interaction remains a low-priority distraction from the term project. I will not let this minor exchange derail my focus on high-impact academic work.
My 1.5-hour effort yielded only 0.12 progress, confirming that the afternoon is slipping while the deadline looms; I must treat this inefficiency as a critical risk to my long-term stress management.

My boundary message to cohort_3 is now active, effectively shielding my remaining energy from social drain. With stress rising and progress lagging, I must maintain this isolation until 17:45 to secure the term project's critical milestones.
My progress rate of 0.12 per hour confirms the afternoon was inefficient; I must recalibrate my approach to meet the deadline before stress escalates further.
My 1.5-hour block yielded only 0.11 progress, confirming my current efficiency is critically low despite the deadline looming.
Despite the two-hour block, my output remains sluggish with only 0.10 progress; this inefficiency is unacceptable given my low energy and the looming deadline.
The trivial chore failure is irrelevant; my 0.06 progress in 1.5 hours remains critically insufficient for the Term project deadline.
My 1-hour output of 0.04 progress is still insufficient to meet the deadline, and my near-zero energy confirms a critical performance failure.
My progress finally accelerated to 0.72, yet zero energy confirms I am running on fumes; this efficiency gain is unsustainable without immediate rest.

desires: Secure immediate, non-negotiable rest to eliminate the zero-energy state that is actively sabotaging my long-term output capacity.
Recover from the critical performance failure of the last 12 hours by establishing a sustainable recovery protocol before the deadline looms closer.
Eliminate the lingering guilt and stress regarding the trivial chore failure, as it represents an unacceptable inefficiency in my resource allocation.
Renegotiate the timeline for the Term project if necessary, but only after ensuring my physical stamina is restored to prevent future catastrophic drops in progress.

goals.description: Immediately execute a 90-minute physiological recovery block (sleep or total rest) to reset the zero-energy state and prevent catastrophic cognitive decline.
goals.rationale: My Achievement axis dictates that output quality is non-negotiable; working at 'zero energy' yields diminishing returns that threaten the long-term ROI of the Term Project. My Deliberate nature recognizes that forcing work now guarantees failure later, so strategic withdrawal is the only efficient path to future success.

goals.description: Formally terminate the 'quick_chore' objective by accepting the missed deadline as a calculated loss of opportunity cost rather than attempting a futile salvage attempt.
goals.rationale: My Assertive value system requires me to cut losses decisively. The chore is a trivial distraction with a low probability of high-value return compared to the critical need for sleep. Continuing to ruminate on this inefficiency wastes the mental bandwidth required for the upcoming recovery.

goals.description: Post-recovery: Initiate a direct renegotiation protocol with 'prof_assignment' regarding the Term Project timeline if the current trajectory (0.72 progress in 1.5h) indicates an unattainable deadline without further extension.
goals.rationale: While I prefer to deliver on commitments, my Deliberate planning horizon demands realistic assessment. If the velocity is insufficient even after recovery, asserting control over the timeline proactively is superior to delivering a sub-par result under pressure. This will be addressed only after physical stamina is restored.
